summary: In this brief acceptance speech for his election as an International Fellow of the Royal Academy of Engineering, Dr. Zhong Lin Wang outlines his two major career contributions: piezoelectric/triboelectric nanogenerators and piezotronics. He highlights their broad applications in IoT, healthcare, and power electronics, emphasizing their long-term importance for human sustainability and the training of future researchers.

principles.statement: Energy harvesting through piezoelectric and triboelectric effects is foundational to long-term human sustainability.
principles.rationale: These technologies enable self-powered systems across wearable electronics, the internet of things, healthcare, environmental protection, and electric automobiles.
principles.source_quote: Those two fields are new and have a longlasting impact to human sustainability.

signature_quotes.text: Those two fields are new and have a longlasting impact to human sustainability.
signature_quotes.context: Referring to his foundational work in triboelectric nanogenerators and piezotronics.
